content:
Phase 2.1 – Firebase Config Integration

Firebase project is now created and Firestore rules are already applied.

Here is the real Firebase configuration:

apiKey: "AIzaSyAgMZNv0rJe7ho36I0_DmjSlgNQm_HN10E"
authDomain: "heavyar-app.firebaseapp.com"
projectId: "heavyar-app"
storageBucket: "heavyar-app.firebasestorage.app"
messagingSenderId: "894313164992"
appId: "1:894313164992:web:ec7ecdea9fa4630d96fd25"

---

TASK:

Integrate this Firebase config into the existing Expo project using the already created service layer.

Use these environment variables:

EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY
EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN
EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID
EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET
EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID
EXPO_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_APP_ID

---

REQUIREMENTS:

- Do NOT rebuild the app
- Do NOT modify UI
- Do NOT change navigation or routes
- Do NOT rename any files
- Only connect Firebase config to existing services

Update:
services/firebaseConfig.ts

Ensure:
- initializeApp is called once
- getAuth() is correctly initialized
- getFirestore() is correctly initialized
- no duplicate Firebase instances

---

AFTER INTEGRATION:

Verify the following:

1. Register (createUserWithEmailAndPassword) works
2. Login works
3. User document is created in Firestore (users collection)
4. Equipment list loads from Firestore
5. Creating equipment works
6. Creating request works
7. Chat real-time listener works
8. Request status updates work
9. No console errors

---

RETURN ONLY:

1. Files modified
2. What is now fully working
3. Any remaining manual steps required (if any)
